current_timeline.description: Buyer reviews planned orders from MRP, manually contacts supplier via email or portal to confirm delivery dates, updates ERP with confirmed delivery schedule
Warehouse team physically receives goods, manually creates goods receipt note in ERP — often delayed due to backlog or missing paperwork from supplier
Quality technician inspects goods against specification, records results in QMS, raises NCR if non-conforming — inspection queue often causes delays
Warehouse team transfers cleared goods to stock location in WMS, updates ERP stock status
AP receives invoice, manually performs three-way match against PO and GRN in ERP, routes for approval, processes payment in next payment run

actors.name: Buyer
actors.involvement: Manages supplier delivery scheduling, resolves delivery exceptions, handles NCR supplier negotiations

actors.name: Warehouse Team
actors.involvement: Physically receives goods, scans and records receipt, transfers stock to storage locations

actors.name: Quality Technician
actors.involvement: Performs physical inspection against specification, makes accept/reject decisions, raises NCRs for non-conforming goods

actors.name: Accounts Payable
actors.involvement: Reviews three-way match results, approves high-value payments, processes payment runs

systems.name: ERP
systems.role: Purchase orders, goods receipts, invoice processing, payment authorization

systems.name: Supplier Portal
systems.role: Supplier-facing portal for delivery scheduling, ASN submission, and invoice upload

systems.name: WMS
systems.role: Warehouse management — goods receipt scanning, location assignment, stock transfer

systems.name: QMS
systems.role: Quality management — inspection plans, results recording, NCR management

data_objects.name: Purchase Order
data_objects.description: Authoritative procurement document — quantity, price, delivery date, terms

data_objects.name: Goods Receipt
data_objects.description: Created at receipt — records actual quantity received, condition, and receipt date

data_objects.name: Invoice
data_objects.description: Supplier invoice — matched against PO and GRN in three-way match for payment authorization

data_objects.name: NCR
data_objects.description: Non-Conformance Record — raised when received goods fail quality inspection

pain_points.title: Delivery Scheduling Delays
pain_points.description: Buyers spend significant time in email exchanges with suppliers to confirm delivery windows — a task that is highly automatable given supplier portal connectivity

pain_points.title: GRN Backlog
pain_points.description: Goods receipt notes are often created hours or days after physical receipt due to manual data entry — creates discrepancy between physical stock and system stock

pain_points.title: Three-Way Match Exceptions
pain_points.description: AP teams spend 70% of invoice processing time on exceptions — price variances, quantity mismatches, missing GRNs — rather than straight-through processing

pain_points.title: Slow Payment Cycle
pain_points.description: Batch payment runs and manual approval chains mean suppliers wait 10–12 days after invoice submission for payment — reduces supplier goodwill and misses early-pay discounts

agent_steps.name: Schedule Deliveries (S1.1)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Fully autonomous for standard delivery scheduling within confirmed lead times. Buyer is notified of booked deliveries and involved only when supplier cannot meet required delivery date.
agent_steps.summary: Reads planned purchase orders from ERP, transmits delivery requests to the supplier portal for each PO, confirms delivery windows against warehouse receiving capacity, and updates ERP with confirmed delivery dates. Flags any supplier delivery promises that miss the required date for Buyer follow-up.

context_data.description: MRP-generated planned orders approved for release — supplier, item, quantity, required delivery date, ship-to location
Supplier-confirmed lead times, available delivery windows, and any capacity constraints communicated via portal
Warehouse inbound dock capacity by day — prevents scheduling more deliveries than the warehouse can receive

skills.name: Delivery Window Optimization
skills.description: Matches supplier available windows against warehouse receiving capacity and required-by date — selects optimal slot that meets need date without exceeding dock capacity

skills.name: ASN Monitoring
skills.description: Tracks Advanced Shipment Notifications from suppliers against booked delivery windows — alerts when ASN is not received 24 hours before expected delivery

skills.name: Supplier Communication
skills.description: Generates structured delivery request messages via supplier portal API — handles acknowledgement parsing and confirmation recording

tools_and_data.purpose: Read planned orders, write confirmed delivery dates
Submit delivery requests and read supplier confirmations
Read dock capacity and book inbound delivery slots

guardrails.description: If supplier cannot confirm delivery by required date, escalate to Buyer immediately — do not accept late delivery without human approval
Never book more deliveries per day than warehouse receiving capacity allows — prevents receiving bottlenecks

outputs.name: Confirmed Delivery Schedule
outputs.description: PO updated with confirmed delivery date and ASN reference — triggers warehouse inbound planning

involved_roles.involvement: Handles escalations when supplier cannot meet required delivery date; approves rescheduling

agent_steps.name: Receive Product (S1.2)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent creates the GRN in ERP immediately upon warehouse team's scan confirmation — the physical receipt is always performed by warehouse staff; agent handles the system recording in real time.
agent_steps.summary: Monitors for inbound delivery arrival notifications, receives scan data from warehouse mobile devices as goods are physically received, creates the Goods Receipt Note in ERP in real time, and records any discrepancies between ASN quantities and actual received quantities. Notifies Quality Agent to initiate inspection.

context_data.description: Barcode/RFID scan events from warehouse team's mobile devices as each pallet/carton is received at the dock
Advance Shipment Notification from supplier — expected items, quantities, lot numbers, and expiry dates
Reference PO for quantity and price verification against what is being received

skills.name: Real-Time GRN Creation
skills.description: Creates Goods Receipt Note in ERP as scan events arrive — line by line, updating quantities in real time rather than in batch at end of receiving

skills.name: Discrepancy Detection
skills.description: Compares scanned quantities against ASN and PO — flags shorts, overs, and substitutions immediately for warehouse team awareness

skills.name: Lot Traceability Recording
skills.description: Records lot numbers and expiry dates from scan data into ERP for full traceability from supplier to internal stock

tools_and_data.purpose: Receive real-time scan events from warehouse mobile devices
Create GRN and update inventory in ERP
Read ASN data for comparison against physical receipt

guardrails.description: GRN can only be created after warehouse team scan confirmation — agent cannot create GRN before physical goods are scanned by a human operator
If received quantity differs from PO quantity by more than 5%, Buyer is notified before GRN is posted — prevents payment for quantities not received

outputs.name: Goods Receipt Note
outputs.description: Posted GRN with actual quantities, lot numbers, and receipt timestamp — triggers quality inspection and three-way match

involved_roles.involvement: Performs physical goods receipt and scanning — mandatory human activity; agent handles only the system recording
Notified of quantity discrepancies; approves or rejects GRN posting when quantities differ significantly

agent_steps.name: Verify Product (S1.3)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent initiates inspection workflow, retrieves spec and inspection plan, and records results digitally as Quality Technician performs inspection. Accept/reject decision always made by Quality Technician; agent drafts NCR if non-conforming.
agent_steps.target_time: < 1 hour (agent tasks); physical inspection time varies by item
agent_steps.summary: Triggers inspection workflow in QMS upon GRN creation, retrieves the inspection plan and specification for the received items, guides Quality Technician through digital checklist, records inspection results, and — if non-conforming — drafts a Non-Conformance Record with classification, containment recommendation, and supplier notification.

context_data.description: Predefined inspection plan for the received item — measurements required, acceptance criteria, sampling plan
Goods receipt details — item, quantity, lot, supplier — from step 2
Historical quality results for this supplier/item combination — informs inspection intensity (skip-lot vs full inspection)

skills.name: Inspection Plan Retrieval
skills.description: Matches received item to correct inspection plan in QMS, determines sampling size based on supplier quality history and AQL level

skills.name: Digital Checklist Guidance
skills.description: Presents inspection steps sequentially to Quality Technician via QMS mobile app — records each measurement as entered

skills.name: NCR Drafting
skills.description: If technician records non-conformance, agent drafts NCR with: defect classification, affected lot, recommended disposition (use-as-is, rework, return), and supplier notification template

tools_and_data.purpose: Retrieve inspection plan, create inspection record, update quality status
Update GRN quality status (released/blocked) based on inspection outcome

guardrails.description: Quality Technician must record the accept/reject decision — agent records results but cannot make the disposition determination for safety-critical or regulated items
Any non-conforming lot is immediately placed on blocked stock in ERP — agent cannot release non-conforming goods to unrestricted stock without Quality Engineer approval

outputs.name: Inspection Record
outputs.description: Completed inspection with measurements, pass/fail result, technician ID, and timestamp — linked to GRN and lot

outputs.name: NCR
outputs.description: Non-Conformance Record for failed lots — defect classification, containment action, supplier notification, disposition decision

involved_roles.involvement: Performs physical inspection and records results via QMS mobile app; makes accept/reject determination

agent_steps.name: Transfer Product (S1.4)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Fully autonomous for location assignment and WMS transfer order creation once quality clearance is received. Warehouse team physically moves the goods; agent handles system transactions.
agent_steps.summary: Upon quality clearance, assigns the received goods to the optimal storage location in WMS based on item classification, velocity, and available space, creates the internal transfer order in WMS and ERP, and updates unrestricted stock status. Physical movement is performed by warehouse team following WMS-directed put-away.

context_data.description: Available storage locations by zone, capacity, and item compatibility — used to optimize put-away location assignment
Quality release flag from step 3 — only released lots can be transferred to unrestricted stock
Item-level storage requirements — temperature, hazard class, FIFO/FEFO rules, weight limits

skills.name: Put-Away Optimization
skills.description: Assigns optimal storage location based on item velocity (fast-movers near dispatch), storage requirements, available space, and FIFO/FEFO rules

skills.name: WMS Transfer Order Creation
skills.description: Creates directed put-away task in WMS for warehouse team with source location (receiving dock), destination, and quantity

skills.name: ERP Stock Update
skills.description: Moves stock from quality inspection to unrestricted stock in ERP after quality clearance — triggers MRP availability

tools_and_data.purpose: Query available locations, create put-away transfer orders
Post stock transfer from QI to unrestricted

guardrails.description: Stock transfer to unrestricted can only execute after confirmed quality release — agent validates QI status before posting transfer
Location assignment must respect item storage requirements — hazardous goods cannot be placed in non-compliant zones

outputs.name: Stock Transfer
outputs.description: Goods moved to unrestricted stock in ERP and to storage location in WMS — available for MRP and order fulfillment

involved_roles.involvement: Physically moves goods from receiving dock to assigned storage location following WMS directed put-away instructions

agent_steps.name: Authorize Payment (S1.5)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Three-way match and payment instruction generation are fully autonomous for matched invoices. AP approval required for: payments above financial threshold, invoices with price variance >2%, and first-time supplier payments.
agent_steps.target_time: < 1 hour (agent processing); AP approval within 4 hours
agent_steps.summary: Receives supplier invoices (via portal or email/OCR), performs automated three-way match against PO and GRN, screens supplier against sanctions lists, generates payment instruction for matched invoices, and routes exceptions to Accounts Payable. Captures early payment discount opportunities where applicable.

context_data.description: Supplier-submitted invoice — line items, quantities, unit prices, total, payment terms, bank details
Reference PO for agreed price and quantity; GRN for confirmed received quantity — inputs to three-way match
OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists — supplier and bank account screened before every payment

skills.name: Invoice OCR & Parsing
skills.description: Extracts structured line-item data from PDF or portal invoices using OCR and LLM — handles varied invoice formats across supplier base

skills.name: Three-Way Match
skills.description: Matches invoice quantity and price against PO and GRN — identifies matched lines, price variances, and quantity discrepancies

skills.name: Sanctions Screening
skills.description: Screens supplier name and bank account details against current sanctions lists before generating payment instruction — blocks and escalates on any match

skills.name: Payment Instruction Generation
skills.description: Generates payment instruction in ERP with correct amount, payment method, bank details, and value date — respects payment terms and captures early-pay discounts

tools_and_data.purpose: Read electronic invoices submitted via portal
Three-way match, payment instruction creation, approval routing
Screen supplier and bank against sanctions lists

guardrails.description: Every payment instruction must pass sanctions screening — no payment can be generated or approved without a clean sanctions check result logged
Payment can only be authorized when invoice matches PO price within 2% and invoice quantity matches GRN quantity within 1% — exceptions route to AP
Payment instructions above the defined threshold (default: company-specific) require Accounts Payable Manager approval before execution
First payment to a new supplier or new bank account requires AP manual verification of bank details — prevents fraud via account takeover

outputs.name: Payment Instruction
outputs.description: Authorized payment instruction with amount, supplier bank details, value date, and payment reference — queued for next payment run

outputs.name: Match Exception Report
outputs.description: For non-matching invoices: discrepancy details, matched/unmatched lines, recommended resolution — routed to Accounts Payable

involved_roles.involvement: Resolves three-way match exceptions; approves high-value payments; verifies bank details for new suppliers

orchestration.description: Five agents execute in a linear pipeline triggered by planned order release from MRP. Steps 2 and 4 require physical human activity (goods receipt, physical transfer) making this a human-in-the-loop pipeline for physical steps with autonomous system processing wrapping each physical action. Financial authorization gates apply at step 5.
orchestration.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram participant MRP as ERP MRP participant SA as Schedule Agent participant BUY as Buyer participant WH as Warehouse Team participant RA as Receive Agent participant QT as Quality Technician participant VA as Verify Agent participant TA as Transfer Agent participant PA as Payment Agent participant AP as Accounts Payable MRP->>SA: Planned order released Note over SA: Submit to supplier portal
Book delivery slot alt Supplier cannot meet date SA->>BUY: Escalate late delivery BUY->>SA: Approved reschedule end SA->>RA: Delivery confirmed WH->>RA: Goods scanned at dock Note over RA: Create GRN in ERP
Record lot/quantity RA->>VA: GRN created Note over VA: Retrieve inspection plan QT->>VA: Inspection results recorded alt Non-conforming VA->>VA: Draft NCR + block lot end VA->>TA: Quality clearance Note over TA: Assign location
Create WMS transfer order WH->>TA: Goods moved + confirmed TA->>PA: Stock in unrestricted Note over PA: Parse invoice
Sanctions screen
Three-way match alt Match exception or high value PA->>AP: Exception for review AP->>PA: Approved end PA->>PA: Execute payment

handoffs.trigger: Delivery confirmed and booked in WMS
handoffs.payload: PO reference, confirmed delivery date, ASN reference, booked dock slot

handoffs.trigger: GRN posted in ERP
handoffs.payload: GRN number, item, quantity, lot numbers, receipt timestamp

handoffs.trigger: Quality decision recorded (pass or fail disposition)
handoffs.payload: Lot quality status, inspection record reference, disposition decision

handoffs.trigger: Stock posted to unrestricted in ERP
handoffs.payload: GRN reference, transferred quantity, storage location, ERP document number

shared_state.name: Procurement Document Set
shared_state.description: The Purchase Order, Goods Receipt Note, and Invoice form the three-way match document set that flows through the pipeline. Each agent enriches and validates this set, culminating in payment authorization.

stages.fields_added: Confirmed delivery date, dock slot, ASN reference
GRN number, actual quantities, lot numbers, receipt timestamp
Inspection result, quality status, NCR reference if applicable
Storage location, unrestricted stock posting, WMS transfer reference
Three-way match result, sanctions check, payment instruction, value date

guardrails_framework.description: Procurement guardrails address the four key risk areas: data quality of procurement documents, financial controls on payments, operational feasibility of receiving and quality processes, and regulatory compliance including sanctions screening and audit trail.

levels.label: Data Integrity
levels.description: Ensures procurement documents are accurate and consistent before downstream processing.
rules: GRN quantity discrepancies >5% vs PO flagged to Buyer before posting
Invoice data must be parseable and complete before three-way match attempt
ASN data reconciled against physical scan before GRN creation
Lot traceability data (lot number, expiry) recorded for all controlled items

levels.label: Financial Controls
levels.description: Prevents unauthorized or incorrect payments and financial exposure from procurement errors.
rules: Three-way match mandatory — invoice quantity must match GRN, invoice price must match PO within 2%
Payment above threshold requires AP Manager approval
First payment to new supplier or new bank account requires manual bank detail verification
Price variances above tolerance routed to Buyer for PO amendment or invoice rejection

levels.label: Operational Controls
levels.description: Ensures physical receiving and quality processes are feasible and properly sequenced.
rules: Delivery scheduling respects warehouse receiving dock capacity
Quality inspection must complete before stock transfer to unrestricted
Non-conforming lots blocked in ERP immediately — cannot be issued to production
Required delivery date breach escalated to Buyer — agent cannot accept late without approval

levels.label: Regulatory Compliance
levels.description: Ensures all procurement transactions comply with trade regulations, sanctions, and quality standards.
rules: Sanctions screening mandatory before every payment instruction — OFAC, EU, UN lists
Quality disposition for safety-critical parts requires Quality Engineer approval
Full audit trail from PO to payment maintained for 7 years
NCRs raised for all non-conformances and supplier notified within 5 business days

human_gates.trigger: Supplier cannot confirm delivery by required date
human_gates.description: Buyer negotiates with supplier and approves revised delivery date

human_gates.trigger: Received quantity differs from PO by more than 5%
human_gates.description: Buyer approves GRN posting with discrepancy or initiates claim process

human_gates.trigger: Inspection result is fail or non-conforming
human_gates.description: Quality team makes accept/reject/rework decision; NCR disposition approved by Quality Engineer for safety-critical items

human_gates.trigger: Invoice above financial threshold, price variance, or new supplier
human_gates.description: AP reviews match result and approves payment instruction before execution

processes.name: Make to Order (SCOR M2)
processes.summary: AI-augmented production execution: schedule production order, issue materials, produce and test, package, stage finished goods — scheduling reduced from 4–8 hrs to <30 min; physical execution and quality unchanged

feasibility_summary.risk_rationale: Physical production and quality inspection require human presence and expertise. Product quality, safety certifications, and batch traceability are at stake — errors can lead to product recalls, regulatory non-compliance, or customer safety incidents.
feasibility_summary.time_current: 4–8 hours (scheduling); production execution time varies by product
feasibility_summary.time_reduction: Scheduling 85% reduction; overall cycle depends on production time
feasibility_summary.autonomy_note: Scheduling and material planning are autonomous; production execution, quality decisions, and packaging sign-off require human operators and quality technicians
feasibility_summary.complexity_rationale: MES integration requires deep shop-floor connectivity; quality decision logic is highly product-specific; regulatory compliance (GMP, ISO) constrains what can be automated

breakdown.note: Capacity + materials + routing + sequencing optimization
BOM explosion + lot selection + pick list generation
Work instruction retrieval + test spec lookup + result recording
Packaging spec lookup + label generation
Location assignment + shipping document generation

key_risks: Product quality — incorrect production parameters or skipped quality steps can produce unsafe products
Safety certifications — regulatory compliance (GMP, ISO 9001) requires documented human oversight at critical steps
Batch traceability — lot genealogy must be complete from raw material to finished goods for recall capability
Scheduling errors — wrong material allocation or capacity assignment ripples to customer delivery commitments
key_benefits: Scheduling time reduced from 4–8 hours to <30 minutes — faster response to urgent orders
Optimal material lot selection (FEFO, quality grade) applied consistently rather than by operator judgment
Digital work instructions reduce transcription errors and improve first-pass quality
Real-time production progress visibility enables early detection of delays
Complete digital audit trail for regulatory compliance and customer quality documentation
feasibility_summary.ai_process_mermaid:
graph LR
  subgraph SchedAgent["Scheduling Agent"]
    direction LR
    sa1["Load Order"]:::agent --> sa2["Optimize Schedule"]:::agent --> sa3["Resolve Conflicts"]:::agent --> sa4["Release"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph MatAgent["Material Agent"]
    direction LR
    ma1["Generate Picks"]:::agent --> ma2["Check Availability"]:::agent --> ma3["Direct Staging"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph Oper["Operator"]
    direction LR
    o1["Execute Production"]:::human --> o2["Record at Terminal"]:::human
  end
  subgraph QualAgent["Quality Agent"]
    direction LR
    qa1["Monitor In-process"]:::agent --> qa2["Flag Anomalies"]:::agent --> qa3["Recommend Disposition"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph QualEng["Quality Engineer"]
    direction LR
    qe1["Final Inspection"]:::human --> qe2["Release Decision"]:::human
  end
  sa4 --> ma1
  ma3 --> o1
  o2 --> qa1
  qa3 --> qe1
  classDef agent fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,color:#166534,font-weight:bold
  classDef human fill:#f3e8ff,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#5b21b6

process_overview.name: Make to Order (SCOR M2)
process_overview.description: End-to-end make-to-order production execution mapping to SCOR M2.1–M2.5: schedule production activities, issue materials to the production floor, produce and test the product, package the finished product, and stage finished goods for shipment. AI agents handle scheduling, material planning, work instruction delivery, and documentation; physical production, quality inspection, and packaging sign-off remain human activities. High-risk process due to product safety, regulatory compliance, and batch traceability requirements.
process_overview.mermaid:
graph LR
  subgraph Sched["Production Scheduler"]
    direction LR
    s1["Load Order"]:::lane1 --> s2["Check Capacity"]:::lane1 --> s3["Check Materials"]:::lane1 --> s4["Release to Floor"]:::lane1
  end
  subgraph WH["Warehouse"]
    direction LR
    w1["Generate Pick List"]:::lane2 --> w2["Pick Materials"]:::lane2 --> w3["Issue to Production"]:::lane2
  end
  subgraph Oper["Operator"]
    direction LR
    o1["Execute Production"]:::lane3 --> o2["In-process Checks"]:::lane3 --> o3["Record Actuals"]:::lane3 --> o4["Package"]:::lane3
  end
  subgraph QA["Quality"]
    direction LR
    q1["Final Inspection"]:::lane4 --> q2["Release / Reject"]:::lane4
  end
  s4 --> w1
  w3 --> o1
  o4 --> q1
  classDef lane1 fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,color:#1e40af
  classDef lane2 fill:#f3e8ff,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#5b21b6
  classDef lane3 fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  classDef lane4 fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,color:#166534

current_timeline.description: Production Scheduler manually reviews work center capacity, checks material availability, sequences orders to minimize changeovers, and releases the schedule to the floor
Warehouse operator picks materials against paper or system pick list, records lot selection manually, delivers to work center — lot selection often suboptimal
Operator performs production following paper work instructions; quality technician performs in-process and final testing; results recorded manually in QMS
Operator packages finished product per packaging specification; labels generated and applied; packaging record created manually
Finished goods transferred to staging area in WMS; inventory updated; shipping documentation prepared manually

actors.name: Production Scheduler
actors.involvement: Approves production schedule, resolves capacity conflicts, prioritizes orders when resources are constrained

actors.name: Warehouse Operator
actors.involvement: Picks materials from warehouse following pick list, records lot selection, delivers to production work center

actors.name: Quality Technician
actors.involvement: Performs in-process and final product testing, records results, makes pass/fail determination, signs off batch record

systems.name: MES
systems.role: Manufacturing Execution System — work orders, work instructions, production data collection, equipment integration

systems.name: ERP
systems.role: Production orders, BOM, routing, capacity planning, inventory

systems.name: WMS
systems.role: Warehouse management — material picking, staging, finished goods receipting

systems.name: QMS
systems.role: Quality management — in-process controls, final inspection, batch records, NCR

data_objects.name: Production Order
data_objects.description: Manufacturing work order — product, quantity, required completion date, routing, special requirements

data_objects.name: Bill of Materials
data_objects.description: Component list for the production order — items, quantities, substitutes, quality grades required

data_objects.name: Inspection Record
data_objects.description: Quality test results and batch record — mandatory for regulatory compliance and customer quality documentation

pain_points.title: Scheduling Bottleneck
pain_points.description: Manual scheduling takes 4–8 hours, delaying floor execution. Schedulers must mentally track capacity across multiple work centers, materials availability, and order priorities simultaneously

pain_points.title: Suboptimal Lot Selection
pain_points.description: Warehouse operators often pick the nearest available lot rather than applying FEFO or quality grade rules — leads to expired materials and non-conformances

pain_points.title: Paper-Based Work Instructions
pain_points.description: Operators follow paper instructions that may be out of date — version control is difficult and instruction deviations are not detected until quality review

pain_points.title: Manual Batch Records
pain_points.description: Batch record completion is time-consuming and error-prone; incomplete records delay product release and create regulatory risk

agent_steps.name: Schedule Production (M2.1)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Generates optimized production schedule autonomously; Production Scheduler reviews and approves before release to floor. Scheduler intervenes for capacity conflicts, priority overrides, and complex multi-order sequencing decisions.
agent_steps.summary: Reads production orders from ERP, checks material availability against BOM in WMS, evaluates work center capacity from MES, generates an optimized production schedule minimizing changeovers and meeting customer due dates, and presents to Production Scheduler for approval before releasing to MES.

context_data.description: Open production orders with required completion dates, routing, and priority flags — input to scheduling
Real-time work center status, planned maintenance windows, current queue depth, and setup/changeover times by product family
Available inventory by lot for each BOM component — checks if materials are available to start each order
Component list and routing for each production order — defines material requirements and work center sequence

skills.name: Finite Capacity Scheduling
skills.description: Sequences production orders across work centers respecting capacity limits, setup times, and changeover constraints — optimizes for on-time delivery and changeover minimization

skills.name: Material Availability Check
skills.description: Explodes BOM for each order and checks WMS inventory — identifies material shortages that would prevent order start, flags for Buyer action

skills.name: Due Date Risk Assessment
skills.description: Identifies orders at risk of missing customer due date given current capacity and material availability — prioritizes escalations to Scheduler

tools_and_data.purpose: Read open production orders and write confirmed schedule
Read work center capacity and release schedule to floor
Check component availability for BOM

guardrails.description: Production schedule must be reviewed and approved by Production Scheduler before being released to MES — agent presents draft, human approves
Orders with confirmed material shortages are flagged to Buyer immediately — agent does not schedule orders that cannot be started due to missing materials

involved_roles.involvement: Reviews generated schedule, approves or modifies, resolves conflicts and priority decisions

agent_steps.name: Issue Materials (M2.2)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent generates optimized pick list with FEFO lot selection; Warehouse Operator physically picks and delivers materials. Agent records goods issue in ERP/WMS after operator scan confirmation.
agent_steps.target_time: < 15 minutes (pick list generation); physical picking time varies
agent_steps.summary: Explodes the BOM for each scheduled production order, selects optimal material lots applying FEFO and quality grade rules, generates a directed pick list for the warehouse operator, and records the goods issue in ERP and WMS once the operator confirms pick completion via mobile scan.

context_data.description: Approved production order with BOM explosion — component items, quantities, quality grades required, and work center destination
Available lots by item, location, expiry date, and quality status — used for FEFO lot selection
Quality release status by lot — only released lots can be issued to production

skills.name: FEFO Lot Selection
skills.description: Selects material lots following First-Expiry-First-Out rule within quality grade requirements — minimizes expired material write-offs and ensures product compliance

skills.name: Pick List Generation
skills.description: Creates directed pick list with optimal pick sequence (minimizes travel in warehouse), source location, lot number, quantity, and destination work center

skills.name: Goods Issue Recording
skills.description: Records goods issue in ERP and WMS after warehouse operator scan confirmation — updates inventory positions and production order component consumption

tools_and_data.purpose: Query lot availability and create directed pick task
Post goods issue against production order after pick confirmation
Verify lot quality release status before selection

guardrails.description: Only quality-released lots can be selected and issued to production — blocked or quarantined lots are excluded from pick list regardless of quantity need
Goods issue in ERP only posted after warehouse operator confirms pick via mobile scan — never auto-post without physical confirmation

agent_steps.name: Produce & Test (M2.3)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent delivers digital work instructions, monitors production data from MES, and records test results as Quality Technician enters them. Production execution and quality decisions are always human activities.
agent_steps.target_time: Digital support < 5 minutes; physical production time varies by product
agent_steps.summary: Retrieves and presents digital work instructions for the production order, monitors real-time production data from MES (cycle times, process parameters), guides Quality Technician through digital inspection checklist, records in-process and final test results, and creates the batch record. Flags any process parameter deviations or test failures for immediate human attention.

context_data.description: Current approved work instruction version for the product; real-time process parameter data from equipment (temperature, pressure, cycle count)
In-process and final inspection plan with acceptance criteria for the product/customer combination
Production order details including customer specifications, special requirements, and quality plan reference

skills.name: Digital Work Instruction Delivery
skills.description: Retrieves current approved work instruction version from MES and presents step-by-step via operator terminal — prevents use of outdated paper instructions

skills.name: Process Parameter Monitoring
skills.description: Monitors real-time equipment data from MES against specification limits — alerts operator immediately if parameter drifts outside control limits

skills.name: Batch Record Compilation
skills.description: Automatically populates batch record from MES production data and QMS test results — reduces manual transcription and ensures completeness

tools_and_data.purpose: Read work instructions, collect production data, update order status
Retrieve inspection plan, record test results, update batch record
Post production completion and yield to ERP

guardrails.description: Pass/fail determination on in-process and final tests must be made by Quality Technician — agent records results but cannot determine disposition
Any MES parameter outside specification limits triggers immediate alert to operator and records deviation in batch record — production must pause until deviation assessed
Agent presents only the current approved version of work instructions from MES — outdated versions are blocked; version change requires QA approval

agent_steps.name: Package Product (M2.4)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent retrieves packaging specification, generates labels, and creates the packaging record. Physical packaging is performed by the operator; agent handles label accuracy and documentation.
agent_steps.target_time: < 10 minutes (agent tasks); physical packaging time varies
agent_steps.summary: Retrieves the packaging specification for the product/customer combination, generates accurate product labels with batch number, expiry, and required regulatory markings, guides the operator through the packaging checklist, and creates the completed packaging record linked to the batch record.

context_data.description: Customer-specific packaging requirements — pack size, label format, regulatory markings required (CE, FDA, etc.), inner/outer box configuration
Confirmed batch number, quantity, expiry date, and test release status from step 3 — input to label generation

skills.name: Packaging Spec Retrieval

Generate and validate product labels; record packaging batch

guardrails.description: Packaging can only proceed for batches with confirmed quality release from step 3 — blocked batches cannot be packaged

agent_steps.summary: Receives packaged goods into WMS finished goods location, assigns the optimal staging location based on planned shipment date and dock assignment, creates the finished goods inventory record in ERP, and generates shipping documentation (packing list, COA) for the customer order.

Generate Certificate of Analysis from batch record

orchestration.description: Five agents support a human-intensive production process. Steps 1 and 2 are AI-led (scheduling, pick list) with human approval; steps 3 and 4 wrap human physical activities with digital support; step 5 is autonomous. The pipeline is sequential and event-driven — each agent triggers on completion of the previous step's output.
orchestration.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram participant ERP as ERP participant ScA as Schedule Agent participant PS as Production Scheduler participant MA as Materials Agent participant WH as Warehouse Operator participant PA as Production Agent participant QT as Quality Technician participant PKA as Package Agent participant STA as Stage Agent ERP->>ScA: Production order created Note over ScA: Check materials + capacity
Generate schedule ScA->>PS: Draft schedule for approval PS->>ScA: Approved ScA->>MA: Schedule released Note over MA: BOM explosion
FEFO lot selection
Generate pick list MA->>WH: Directed pick list WH->>MA: Scan confirmation MA->>PA: Goods issued to floor Note over PA: Deliver work instructions
Monitor parameters PA->>QT: Inspection checklist QT->>PA: Test results recorded PA->>PKA: Batch record complete Note over PKA: Retrieve spec
Generate labels PKA->>WH: Packaging instructions WH->>PKA: Packaging complete PKA->>STA: Packaged goods ready Note over STA: FG receipt
Generate COA + packing list STA->>ERP: Finished goods available

shared_state.name: Production Order Lifecycle
shared_state.description: The production order in ERP/MES accumulates data through each step — from released schedule through materials issued, production confirmed, and finished goods receipted — creating a complete traceability chain from raw material lots to finished goods shipment.

stages.fields_added: Work center assignment, planned start/end, priority
Component lot numbers, goods issue document, actual quantities issued
Actual yield, process parameters, test results, batch record reference
Label batch, packaging materials, packaging record reference
FG location, COA reference, packing list, ready-to-ship flag

guardrails_framework.description: Manufacturing guardrails reflect the high-risk nature of physical production. Data integrity ensures inputs are correct; financial controls prevent material waste; operational controls keep the floor safe and compliant; compliance controls meet regulatory requirements for batch records and quality documentation.

levels.label: Data Integrity
levels.description: Ensures all production data is accurate and traceable from raw material to finished good.
rules: Goods issue only posted after warehouse scan confirmation — no auto-posting without physical confirmation
Batch record must be complete before finished goods can be staged for shipment
COA data validated against batch record before generating customer-facing document
Lot genealogy chain must be unbroken from component to finished goods

levels.label: Financial Controls
levels.description: Prevents material waste and production cost overruns.
rules: Material shortage alerts issued before production starts — no mid-production material shortfall
Yield deviations above threshold flagged for Production Scheduler review
FEFO lot selection mandatory — prevents expired material write-offs
Packaging material consumption recorded for accurate costing

levels.label: Operational Safety
levels.description: Ensures production operations are safe and compliant with work instructions.
rules: Only current approved work instruction version can be presented to operator
Process parameter deviations trigger immediate production stop alert
Non-conforming batches blocked immediately — cannot proceed to packaging without QE disposition
Production Scheduler approval required before floor schedule release

levels.label: Regulatory Compliance
levels.description: Meets GMP, ISO 9001, and customer quality system requirements.
rules: Quality Technician must make pass/fail determination — agent records, human decides
Batch record completeness check mandatory before FG staging
Packaging for regulated markets requires QA-approved label templates
Complete batch documentation retained for minimum regulatory retention period

human_gates.trigger: Before every floor schedule release
human_gates.description: Production Scheduler reviews and approves generated schedule before it is released to MES

human_gates.trigger: In-process or final test failure
human_gates.description: Quality Technician makes pass/fail decision; Quality Engineer approves disposition for safety-critical failures

human_gates.trigger: MES process parameter outside specification limits
human_gates.description: Operator assesses deviation and decides whether to continue, adjust, or stop production

processes.name: Deliver Stocked Product (SCOR D1)
processes.summary: Order-to-ship automation: validate order, reserve stock and plan shipment, coordinate pick/pack/ship, invoice — order-to-ship reduced from 2–3 days to same-day for in-stock items with 75% autonomous handling

feasibility_summary.risk_rationale: Customer experience risk from wrong shipments; compliance risk from export controls on certain product/destination combinations. Physical pick/pack always requires warehouse staff. Otherwise highly automatable.
feasibility_summary.autonomy_note: Order validation, ATP check, inventory reservation, carrier selection, and invoicing are fully autonomous; physical pick/pack requires warehouse staff; export compliance review needed for controlled products
feasibility_summary.complexity_rationale: ERP, WMS, and TMS APIs are mature; main complexity is export compliance logic by product/destination and EDI integration with carrier networks

breakdown.note: Order data + customer credit check + ATP + export screening
Inventory reservation + carrier selection + route optimization + delivery date commitment
Pick list generation + BOL creation + carrier tendering — mostly structured
Invoice generation + revenue recognition + payment terms + e-invoice transmission

key_risks: Wrong shipment — incorrect product or quantity damages customer relationship and creates costly returns
Export controls — shipping controlled products to embargoed destinations without screening is a serious regulatory violation
Credit exposure — shipping to customers with overdue balances or exceeded credit limits
Carrier performance — autonomous carrier selection must account for reliability, not just cost
key_benefits: Same-day shipment for in-stock orders eliminates 2–3 day processing delay
Automated ATP prevents promising delivery dates that cannot be met
Consistent export compliance screening on every order regardless of volume
Invoice immediately upon shipment confirmation — accelerates cash collection
Real-time order status visibility for customer and CSR without manual tracking

process_overview.name: Deliver Stocked Product (SCOR D1)
process_overview.description: End-to-end order fulfillment for stocked products mapping to SCOR D1: receive and validate the customer order, reserve inventory and plan the shipment, coordinate pick/pack/ship with the warehouse, and invoice the customer. Currently takes 2–3 days due to manual order entry, ATP checks, pick list creation, and invoice generation. The target state uses four AI agents to achieve same-day shipment for in-stock items, with humans involved only for physical warehouse operations and exception cases.

graph LR
  subgraph CSRLane["CSR"]
    direction LR
    c1["Receive Order"]:::lane1 --> c2["Validate"]:::lane1 --> c3["Credit Check"]:::lane1 --> c4["ATP Check"]:::lane1 --> c5["Confirm"]:::lane1
  end

    direction LR
    w1["Reserve Inventory"]:::lane2 --> w2["Create Pick Wave"]:::lane2 --> w3["Pick"]:::lane2 --> w4["Pack"]:::lane2 --> w5["Stage"]:::lane2
  end

    direction LR
    l1["Select Carrier"]:::lane3 --> l2["Book Shipment"]:::lane3 --> l3["Generate Docs"]:::lane3 --> l4["Load"]:::lane3 --> l5["Ship"]:::lane3
  end
  subgraph Finance["Finance"]
    direction LR
    f1["Generate Invoice"]:::lane4 --> f2["Apply Pricing"]:::lane4 --> f3["Transmit"]:::lane4 --> f4["Post AR"]:::lane4
  end

current_timeline.description: CSR manually enters or reviews order in ERP, checks ATP, verifies customer credit status, and confirms delivery date — often delayed by credit holds and ATP mismatches
Logistics Coordinator reserves inventory, selects carrier, books transport, and generates pick list — carrier selection often based on habit rather than optimized cost/service trade-off
Warehouse team picks and packs order, creates BOL, dispatches with carrier — BOL creation and carrier tendering often manual and error-prone
Invoice generated after shipping confirmation, often in next-day batch run — delays cash collection and creates dispute risk if invoice differs from customer PO

actors.involvement: Handles order entry exceptions, customer credit disputes, and special delivery requirements

actors.name: Warehouse Picker
actors.involvement: Physically picks products, packs orders, applies labels, hands off to carrier

actors.name: Logistics Coordinator
actors.involvement: Handles carrier exceptions, special freight requirements, and export compliance escalations

systems.name: WMS
systems.role: Inventory reservation, directed picking, packing confirmation, shipment creation

systems.name: TMS
systems.role: Transport management — carrier selection, rate shopping, booking, BOL generation, tracking

systems.name: EDI
systems.role: Electronic data interchange with carriers and customers — ASN, BOL, invoice transmission

data_objects.name: Sales Order
data_objects.description: Customer purchase order translated to sales order in ERP — product, quantity, price, delivery date, shipping terms

data_objects.name: Bill of Lading
data_objects.description: Carrier freight document — shipment details, products, quantities, consignee, terms of carriage

data_objects.name: Invoice
data_objects.description: Customer invoice generated upon shipment confirmation — matches sales order price and shipped quantity

pain_points.title: Order Entry Delays
pain_points.description: Manual order entry and validation takes hours — EDI orders arrive instantly but still require manual review for exceptions before processing

pain_points.title: ATP Promise Inaccuracy
pain_points.description: Manual ATP checks lead to over-promising on delivery dates — customers receive revised dates after order confirmation, damaging trust

pain_points.title: Carrier Selection Inefficiency
pain_points.description: Logistics coordinators often use preferred carriers by habit rather than optimizing cost/service trade-off for each shipment — significant freight cost savings potential

pain_points.title: Invoice Cycle Delay
pain_points.description: Batch invoice runs the day after shipment delay payment collection by 1–2 days per order — at 5,000 orders/year this is material working capital impact

agent_steps.name: Receive & Validate Order (D1.1–D1.3)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Fully autonomous for standard orders passing all validation checks. CSR involved only for credit holds, export compliance flags, or order anomalies. Export Compliance Officer handles controlled product screening exceptions.
agent_steps.summary: Receives inbound sales orders via ERP, EDI, or customer portal, validates order completeness and pricing against contract, performs ATP check to confirm available inventory and delivery date, screens against export control lists for controlled products, checks customer credit status, and confirms the order to the customer with a committed delivery date.

context_data.description: Sales order data — customer, products, quantities, requested delivery date, shipping terms, purchase order reference
Customer contract pricing, special terms, volume discounts, and approved product list for this customer
Real-time ATP by SKU and location — confirms if requested quantity is available for the requested delivery date
Restricted party lists and product classification (ECCN/HTS) for export screening — required for international shipments
Customer credit limit, outstanding balance, and payment history — determines if order can be processed without credit hold

skills.name: Order Validation
skills.description: Validates order completeness, pricing against contract, and product/customer combination — flags discrepancies for CSR review

skills.name: ATP Check
skills.description: Performs real-time available-to-promise check in ERP — confirms quantity, location, and delivery date feasibility; proposes alternative date if requested date unavailable

skills.name: Export Compliance Screening
skills.description: Screens customer, end-use destination, and product against export control lists — blocks and escalates to Export Compliance Officer for controlled items

skills.name: Order Confirmation
skills.description: Generates order acknowledgement to customer with confirmed delivery date, price, and tracking reference — sent via email or EDI

tools_and_data.purpose: Read inbound order, create sales order, perform ATP check
Receive EDI orders and send order acknowledgement
Screen restricted party and product classification

guardrails.description: Every international order must pass export compliance screening before order confirmation — flagged orders are held and escalated to Export Compliance Officer
Orders that would exceed customer credit limit are placed on hold and routed to CSR — agent cannot override credit limits
Delivery date is committed to customer only after ATP check confirms availability — never promise a date that cannot be met

outputs.description: Validated and confirmed sales order with committed delivery date, credit status cleared, export screening passed

outputs.name: Order Acknowledgement
outputs.description: Order confirmation sent to customer with delivery date, price, and order reference

involved_roles.involvement: Resolves credit holds, pricing disputes, and order anomalies flagged by agent
Export Compliance Officer consulted for controlled product export screening exceptions

agent_steps.name: Reserve Stock & Plan Shipment (D1.4–D1.6)

skills.name: Pick List Generation

tools_and_data.purpose: Reserve inventory and create outbound delivery
Get carrier rates and book shipment
Create outbound delivery document linked to sales order

Selected carrier transit time must guarantee delivery by committed date — agent escalates to Logistics Coordinator if no carrier can meet the commitment

orchestration.description: Four agents execute sequentially from order receipt to invoice transmission. Steps 1 and 2 are fully autonomous for clean orders; step 3 wraps physical warehouse activities; step 4 is autonomous. The pipeline is event-driven and can complete end-to-end in under an hour for in-stock standard orders, enabling same-day shipment.

shared_state.name: Order Fulfillment Lifecycle
shared_state.description: The sales order in ERP accumulates status and documents through each step — from received through confirmed, picked, shipped, and invoiced — providing complete order visibility and traceability.

stages.fields_added: Credit status, export screening result, ATP confirmation, committed delivery date
Reserved lots, carrier selection, TMS booking reference, ASN sent
Goods issue document, BOL reference, carrier tracking number, actual ship date
Invoice number, invoice amount, transmission confirmation, AR due date

guardrails_framework.description: Order fulfillment guardrails protect customer experience, financial accuracy, regulatory compliance, and operational integrity. The primary risks are wrong shipments, incorrect invoices, and export control violations.

levels.label: Data Integrity
levels.description: Ensures order data flows accurately from receipt to invoice without errors.
rules: Goods issue only posted after warehouse pack-complete scan — no auto-posting
BOL quantities validated against outbound delivery before carrier transmission
Invoice quantity must equal shipped quantity — partial shipments invoiced separately
Lot traceability recorded from reservation through goods issue for every controlled item

levels.label: Financial Controls
levels.description: Prevents incorrect invoices, unauthorized credit extensions, and revenue recognition errors.
rules: Invoice price must come from validated sales order — no deviations without CSR approval
Customer credit limit enforced at order validation — no override without CSR approval
Tax amounts validated before invoice transmission
Revenue recognition posted per accounting policy — no manual override without finance approval

levels.label: Operational Quality
levels.description: Maintains delivery reliability and customer experience standards.
rules: Delivery date committed only after ATP check — never over-promise
Carrier selection must guarantee committed delivery date — escalate if no carrier can comply
Reserved inventory cannot be allocated to a different order without system approval
ASN sent to customer when shipment is booked — keeps customer informed throughout

levels.label: Export & Regulatory Compliance
levels.description: Ensures all international shipments comply with export regulations and trade controls.
rules: Export compliance screening mandatory for all international orders before confirmation
Controlled products (ECCN-classified) require Export Compliance Officer review for restricted destinations
BOL must include all mandatory customs fields for international shipments
Export documentation retained for 5 years per export control regulations

human_gates.trigger: Order would exceed customer credit limit or customer has overdue balance
human_gates.description: CSR reviews credit situation and either releases hold, requests prepayment, or rejects order

human_gates.trigger: Order contains controlled products or ships to restricted/embargoed destination
human_gates.description: Export Compliance Officer reviews and approves or blocks the shipment

human_gates.trigger: No carrier can meet committed delivery date or special freight requirements
human_gates.description: Logistics Coordinator selects alternative carrier, negotiates special handling, or contacts customer to revise delivery date

processes.name: Return Defective to Supplier (SCOR SR1)
processes.summary: Defect-to-credit automation: identify defect, obtain RMA, ship return, receive replacement, settle financially — cycle reduced from 15–30 days to 8–12 days with 45% autonomous handling

feasibility_summary.risk_rationale: Quality risk from wrong disposition of safety-critical parts; financial risk from incorrect credit amounts. Documentation and financial settlement are highly automatable; defect assessment and supplier negotiation require human expertise.
feasibility_summary.autonomy_note: NCR creation, RMA tracking, shipment coordination, and credit matching are autonomous; defect assessment and accept/reject decisions require Quality Engineer; supplier disputes need Buyer involvement
feasibility_summary.complexity_rationale: QMS and supplier portal APIs available; main complexity is varying supplier RMA processes and credit note matching logic across diverse supplier base

breakdown.note: NCR data + defect classification + supplier quality history + root cause hypothesis
RMA request generation + supplier communication + confirmation tracking
Return shipment documentation + carrier booking
Replacement receipt and quality check initiation
Credit note matching + three-way reconciliation + payment instruction

key_risks: Wrong disposition — accepting defective safety-critical parts back into stock without proper assessment creates product safety risk
Financial: incorrect credits — credit note amount may not match return value; requires careful three-way reconciliation
Supplier disputes — contested defect liability requires Buyer and Quality Engineer negotiation with supplier
Traceability gaps — incomplete lot documentation can prevent supplier from accepting return or claiming under warranty
key_benefits: 50% return cycle reduction (15–30 days → 8–12 days) through automated NCR, RMA, and credit matching
NCR created immediately on defect identification — no delay waiting for Buyer to initiate
Automated RMA follow-up eliminates chasing suppliers manually
Credit note matching catches financial discrepancies before AP posts payment
Supplier quality KPIs tracked automatically from every NCR — supports sourcing decisions
feasibility_summary.ai_process_mermaid:
graph LR
  subgraph NCRAgent["NCR Agent"]
    direction LR
    na1["Generate NCR"]:::agent --> na2["Auto-quarantine"]:::agent --> na3["Assess Scope"]:::agent --> na4["Recommend Disposition"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph RMAAgent["RMA Agent"]
    direction LR
    ra1["Draft Notification"]:::agent --> ra2["Track RMA"]:::agent --> ra3["Monitor Terms"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph LogAgent["Logistics Agent"]
    direction LR
    la1["Generate Return Docs"]:::agent --> la2["Book Carrier"]:::agent --> la3["Track Shipment"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph SettAgent["Settlement Agent"]
    direction LR
    sa1["Match Credit Note"]:::agent --> sa2["Validate Amounts"]:::agent --> sa3["Post"]:::agent --> sa4["Update Scorecard"]:::agent
  end
  subgraph QELane["Quality Engineer"]
    direction LR
    qe1["Confirm Disposition"]:::human
  end
  subgraph BuyerLane["Buyer"]
    direction LR
    by1["Resolve Dispute"]:::human
  end
  na4 -.->|safety-critical| qe1
  na4 --> ra1
  ra1 -.->|supplier rejects| by1
  ra3 --> la1
  la3 --> sa1
  classDef agent fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,color:#166534,font-weight:bold
  classDef human fill:#f3e8ff,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#5b21b6

process_overview.name: Return Defective to Supplier (SCOR SR1)
process_overview.description: End-to-end supplier return process mapping to SCOR SR1.1–SR1.5: identify defective product and create NCR, obtain Return Material Authorization from supplier, ship defective material back to supplier, receive replacement or repair, and complete financial settlement via credit note or replacement PO. Currently a 15–30 day process that depends on manual NCR creation, email exchanges with suppliers for RMA, and manual credit note reconciliation. AI agents automate the documentation, communication tracking, and financial matching while keeping Quality Engineer and Buyer in the loop for technical and commercial decisions.
process_overview.mermaid:
graph LR
  subgraph QE["Quality Engineer"]
    direction LR
    q1["Raise NCR"]:::lane1 --> q2["Quarantine"]:::lane1 --> q3["Assess Scope"]:::lane1 --> q4["Disposition Decision"]:::lane1
  end
  subgraph BuyerLane["Buyer"]
    direction LR
    b1["Contact Supplier"]:::lane2 --> b2["Request RMA"]:::lane2 --> b3["Negotiate Terms"]:::lane2
  end
  subgraph WH["Warehouse"]
    direction LR
    w1["Package Return"]:::lane3 --> w2["Label"]:::lane3 --> w3["Book Carrier"]:::lane3 --> w4["Ship"]:::lane3
  end
  subgraph AP["Accounts Payable"]
    direction LR
    a1["Receive Credit Note"]:::lane4 --> a2["Validate"]:::lane4 --> a3["Post"]:::lane4 --> a4["Close NCR"]:::lane4
  end
  q4 --> b1
  b3 --> w1
  w4 --> a1
  classDef lane1 fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,color:#1e40af
  classDef lane2 fill:#f3e8ff,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#5b21b6
  classDef lane3 fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  classDef lane4 fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,color:#166534

current_timeline.description: Quality Technician discovers defect during inspection or production use; manually creates NCR in QMS; Buyer and Quality Engineer assess return disposition — often delayed by availability
Buyer emails supplier requesting RMA; waits for supplier response; follows up manually; records RMA number in ERP — highly variable supplier response times
Warehouse team picks defective items; Buyer arranges return shipment; creates return delivery documents; ships to supplier — freight booking often manual and delayed
Wait for supplier to send replacement or repair; receive and inspect replacement; Quality Technician re-inspects; records receipt
Supplier issues credit note or replacement PO; AP manually matches credit against original invoice; posts credit or approves replacement PO — reconciliation often error-prone

actors.name: Quality Engineer
actors.involvement: Assesses defect severity, determines return disposition (return vs. use-as-is vs. scrap), approves NCR, inspects replacement

actors.name: Buyer
actors.involvement: Negotiates with supplier on RMA conditions, credit amount, and replacement timeline; escalates disputed returns

actors.name: Warehouse Team
actors.involvement: Physically picks defective goods, packages for return, hands off to carrier for return shipment; receives replacement

actors.name: Accounts Payable
actors.involvement: Processes credit note from supplier — matches against original invoice and posts credit or creates replacement PO

systems.name: WMS

systems.name: Supplier Portal

pain_points.title: Slow NCR Initiation
pain_points.description: NCR creation is manual and often delayed — defective goods sit in blocked stock for days before formal return process is started, extending overall cycle

pain_points.title: RMA Chase Loop
pain_points.description: Buyers spend significant time following up with suppliers on RMA approvals via email — no systematic tracking means items fall through the cracks

pain_points.title: Credit Note Reconciliation Errors
pain_points.description: Manual matching of credit notes against original invoices is error-prone — wrong amounts posted create AP discrepancies that require additional investigation

pain_points.title: Supplier Quality Tracking Gap
pain_points.description: Without automated NCR-to-resolution tracking, supplier quality KPIs are inconsistently maintained — defect trends not surfaced for sourcing decisions

agent_steps.name: Identify Defect & Create NCR (SR1.1)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent creates NCR draft from QMS inspection failure data and retrieves relevant supplier history. Quality Engineer reviews, confirms defect classification, and determines return disposition — agent executes blocking and supplier notification.
agent_steps.target_time: < 30 minutes (agent tasks); Quality Engineer review within 4 hours
agent_steps.summary: Triggered by a failed quality inspection in QMS, the agent creates a structured NCR draft with defect classification, affected lot details, and supplier quality history. Presents to Quality Engineer for disposition decision (return, use-as-is, scrap, rework). Upon decision, blocks the affected lots in ERP/WMS, notifies the supplier, and passes the NCR to the RMA Agent.

context_data.description: Failed inspection record — defect type, affected lot, quantity, inspection date, photos if available
Original PO details for the failed lot — supplier, price, quantity, delivery date
Historical NCRs and quality performance for this supplier/item — provides context for severity assessment

skills.name: NCR Drafting
skills.description: Creates structured NCR from inspection failure data — defect description, classification (critical/major/minor), affected lots and quantities, initial root cause hypothesis based on defect type

skills.name: Supplier History Analysis
skills.description: Retrieves and summarizes this supplier's quality history for the item — flags if this is a recurring defect pattern

skills.name: Lot Blocking
skills.description: Places all affected lots in quality block in ERP and WMS immediately upon defect identification — prevents defective material from being used in production

tools_and_data.purpose: Create NCR record with defect data and supplier reference
Block affected lots in ERP stock
Place affected lots on quality hold in WMS

guardrails.description: All lots identified as potentially defective must be immediately blocked in ERP and WMS — agent does not wait for QE decision before blocking
Return disposition decision (return/use-as-is/scrap) must be made by Quality Engineer — agent cannot determine disposition for safety-critical items

involved_roles.involvement: Reviews NCR draft, confirms defect severity, determines return disposition, approves supplier notification

agent_steps.name: Obtain RMA (SR1.2)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Fully autonomous for RMA request submission and follow-up tracking. Buyer is notified of RMA status and involved only when supplier disputes the defect or proposes unacceptable return conditions.
agent_steps.target_time: < 1 hour (agent tasks); supplier response time varies (target: 2 business days)
agent_steps.summary: Generates and submits the formal RMA request to the supplier via portal or email with full NCR documentation, tracks supplier response and follows up automatically on the defined schedule, records the RMA authorization number in ERP and QMS upon approval, and escalates to Buyer if supplier disputes the defect or exceeds the response SLA.

context_data.description: Confirmed NCR with defect details, lot information, and QE disposition — primary input to RMA request
Purchase order reference, supplier contact details, warranty terms, and return address
Supplier-specific RMA submission format and approval workflow

skills.name: RMA Request Generation
skills.description: Creates formal RMA request document with NCR reference, defect description, affected lot details, quantity to return, and requested resolution (replacement, credit, repair)

skills.name: Supplier Communication Tracking
skills.description: Tracks RMA request status and sends automated follow-up after 2 business days without response — escalates to Buyer at 5 business days

skills.name: RMA Record Creation
skills.description: Records approved RMA number, return conditions, and supplier commitment (replacement timeline or credit amount) in ERP and QMS

tools_and_data.purpose: Submit RMA request and track approval status
Create return purchase order linked to NCR and original PO
Update NCR with RMA number and supplier commitment

guardrails.description: If supplier does not respond to RMA request within 5 business days, automatically escalate to Buyer — do not wait indefinitely
RMA request can only be submitted after QE-approved NCR exists — no RMA without documented defect

agent_steps.decision_logic_mermaid:
graph TD
  A["NCR approved for\nreturn"] --> B["Generate RMA\nrequest"]
  B --> C["Submit to supplier\nvia portal/email"]
  C --> D{"Supplier responds\nwithin 2 days?"}  
  D -->|Yes, approved| E["Record RMA number\nin ERP + QMS"]
  D -->|Yes, disputed| F["Escalate to Buyer\nfor negotiation"]
  D -->|No response| G{"Days since\nrequest?"}
  G -->|<5 days| H["Send follow-up\nmessage"]
  G -->|>=5 days| F
  H --> D
  F --> I["Buyer negotiates\nwith supplier"]
  I --> E
  E --> J["Pass to Shipment Agent"]

  classDef action fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#2563eb,color:#1e40af
  classDef human fill:#ede9fe,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#5b21b6

agent_steps.name: Financial Settlement (SR1.5)
agent_steps.autonomy_description: Agent performs three-way reconciliation and generates payment credit entry. Accounts Payable approves credit posting; Buyer handles discrepancies where credit note amount does not match agreed return value.

agent_steps.summary: Receives supplier credit note or processes replacement as financial settlement, reconciles the credit note amount against the original PO price and return quantity, posts the credit to the supplier account in ERP, and closes the financial loop on the return. orchestration.description: Five agents execute sequentially, with each step gated by supplier actions (RMA approval, replacement shipment) and human decisions (QE disposition, AP approval). The pipeline is not purely time-based — it waits on external supplier events — making automated monitoring and escalation critical to cycle time reduction.

Block lots
Pull supplier history NCRA->>QE: NCR for disposition QE->>NCRA: Approved — return NCRA->>SUPP: Defect notification NCRA->>RMAA: NCR confirmed RMAA->>SUPP: RMA request alt Supplier disputes RMAA->>BUY: Escalate dispute BUY->>SUPP: Negotiates end SUPP->>RMAA: RMA approved RMAA->>SHA: RMA authorized Note over SHA: Create return delivery

shared_state.name: Return Case Lifecycle
shared_state.description: The NCR serves as the shared state document for the entire return process. It is created in step 1 and progressively updated with RMA authorization, return shipment reference, replacement acceptance, and financial settlement — providing complete return traceability.

stages.fields_added: Defect description, affected lots, QE disposition, lot block reference
RMA number, return conditions, supplier commitment (credit/replacement)
Return tracking number, carrier, actual return quantity, ship date
Replacement lot, GRN reference, QE inspection result, resolution date
Credit note reference, credited amount, AP posting reference, total cycle time

guardrails_framework.description: Return process guardrails focus on four areas: data integrity of return documentation, financial accuracy of credit settlement, operational compliance with quality and disposal requirements, and regulatory compliance for cross-border returns and safety-critical parts.

levels.label: Data Integrity
levels.description: Ensures all return documentation is complete and traceable from defect identification through financial closure.
rules: NCR must exist and be QE-approved before RMA request is submitted
Return shipment only initiated after confirmed RMA authorization from supplier
Physical goods handoff required before return shipment recorded in system
Credit note reconciled against original PO price and RMA-authorized quantity before posting

levels.label: Financial Controls
levels.description: Prevents incorrect credit amounts and unauthorized financial postings.
rules: Credit note amount must match expected credit within 2% — discrepancies escalated before posting
AP approval required for all credit note postings to supplier account
Buyer must negotiate and document agreed credit amount when supplier disputes return value
Replacement PO created in ERP for replacement goods — no free goods receipt without PO reference

levels.label: Operational Compliance
levels.description: Ensures quality standards are maintained throughout the return process.
rules: Defective lots blocked immediately upon NCR creation — no production use pending disposition
Quality Engineer must make disposition decision — agent cannot determine return vs. accept
Replacement goods inspected by QE before release to unrestricted stock
Supplier notified of replacement rejection within 1 business day — secondary return initiated

levels.label: Regulatory Compliance
levels.description: Meets regulatory requirements for quality systems, customs, and product safety.
rules: NCR documentation retained per quality system requirements (minimum 10 years for safety-critical parts)
Cross-border returns include required customs documentation and export declarations
Defect root cause recorded in NCR — required for quality system CAPA process
Supplier quality data updated from every NCR for annual supplier qualification review

human_gates.trigger: NCR created for returned/failed goods
human_gates.description: Quality Engineer determines disposition: return, use-as-is with deviation, rework, or scrap

human_gates.trigger: Supplier contests defect liability or rejects RMA
human_gates.description: Buyer negotiates with supplier to reach agreement on return conditions and credit amount

human_gates.trigger: Replacement goods received from supplier
human_gates.description: Quality Engineer inspects and accepts or rejects replacement — determines if return is resolved

human_gates.trigger: Credit note received for posting
human_gates.description: AP reviews reconciliation and approves credit posting to supplier account

methodology_note: These blueprints demonstrate how structured EA Repository data — typed elements, explicit relationships, stable identifiers, and machine-readable constraints — provides exactly the context AI agents need to operate. Each blueprint is grounded in real ArchiMate elements from the Energy Client repository, and the relationships between them (serving, triggering, composing, accessing) are the same typed connections that agents traverse at runtime. Comparing blueprints across domains (safety-critical grid operations vs. routine service operations) reveals how the same structure adapts: the guardrails framework scales from life-safety to data-privacy concerns, the orchestration pattern shifts from tightly-gated to mostly-autonomous, and the human gates move from mandatory step-level approval to exception-based review. For the broader process analysis, see the Process Analysis page.
